name: Rick Brandt
subject: RE: Rick's QA roadmap at Cboe
body:
Hi Rick,

I know my last note wasn't specific enough to be worth your time. Looking at your work leading QA Engineering at Cboe through a migration that still has Selenium and Playwright running side by side, I think this angle is a lot more relevant to what you're actually managing.

I imagine your team is spending sprint time triaging two separate locator break queues, one from the Selenium layer and one from Playwright, instead of building net-new coverage. At Cboe's breadth, that maintenance backlog compounds faster than it clears. If I'm reading that wrong, correct me.

It's the same fragmentation CRED was dealing with. They reached 90% regression coverage 5x faster on Testsigma, not by adding headcount, but because AI self-healing absorbed the locator drift across both layers automatically, so the team stopped context-switching between maintenance cycles.

If that dual-stack maintenance pressure is real for your team right now, would 15 minutes give you a pretty clear picture of whether Testsigma is worth a look?

Rob Gorham
Testsigma

subject: RE: Seth's QA org at Fidelity
body:
Hi Seth,

I know my last note wasn't specific enough to be worth your time. Looking at your VP QA role inside Fidelity's chapter model, where coverage accountability spans dozens of squads rather than one team, I think this angle is a lot more relevant to what you're actually managing.

I imagine the chapter structure makes coverage visibility your hardest problem, not coverage creation. Each squad runs its own framework, reports on its own metrics, and the aggregated picture you need at VP level gets stitched together manually from incompatible sources. If that's not exactly right, set me straight.

It's the same fragmentation Hansard was dealing with. They cut regression cycles from 8 weeks to 5 on Testsigma, and the shift that mattered most to their QA leadership was finally having one reporting layer across all teams instead of reconciling data from multiple tools every sprint.

If that cross-squad visibility gap is real for your organization, would 15 minutes give you a pretty clear picture of whether Testsigma is worth a look?
